items.label: Biological agents (bacteria, viruses, fungi, prions, toxins)
items.helpText: Select if your research involves working with any living organisms or biological toxins, even common laboratory strains.

items.label: Genetic modification or synthetic biology
items.helpText: Includes any alteration of an organism's genetic material, whether by traditional genetic engineering, CRISPR/Cas, or synthetic biology approaches.

items.label: Pathogen characterisation, culturing, or enhancement
items.helpText: Select if you are studying pathogen biology, growing pathogens in culture, or any work that could alter pathogen properties.

items.label: Gain-of-function research
items.helpText: Research that alters an organism to give it new or enhanced capabilities. Examples include engineering a virus to be more transmissible, increasing resistance to treatments, or expanding host range. Even if this is not the primary aim, select this if your methods could produce these effects.

items.label: De novo synthesis of genetic material or whole organisms
items.helpText: Includes both in-house synthesis (e.g., using a benchtop device) and ordering synthetic nucleic acid sequences from commercial providers (e.g., IDT, Twist Bioscience, GenScript).

items.label: Work with human or animal tissue, cells, or biological samples
items.helpText: Includes primary cells, cell lines, tissue samples, blood, and other biological material of human or animal origin.

items.label: Chemical synthesis or processing
items.helpText: Any research involving the creation of chemical compounds through synthetic routes.

items.label: Work with chemical precursors on controlled lists
items.helpText: Chemicals listed on the Chemical Weapons Convention (CWC) Schedules (https://www.opcw.org/chemical-weapons-convention/annexes/annex-chemicals) or the Australia Group chemical precursors list (https://www.dfat.gov.au/publications/minisite/theaustraliagroupnet/site/en/precursors.html).

items.label: Novel toxic compounds or pharmaceutically active substances
items.helpText: Research creating new compounds with significant toxicity or biological activity.

items.label: Aerosol generation, dispersal, or delivery mechanisms
items.helpText: Any work involving aerosolisation, encapsulation, spray drying, or other methods of dispersing substances. Includes drug delivery research.

items.label: Work with explosives, propellants, or energetic materials
Autonomous systems, AI, or machine learning
Robotics or autonomous platforms (drones, unmanned vehicles, robotic manipulation)
Dual-use equipment (fermenters, centrifuges, spray dryers, etc.)
Nuclear or radiological materials
Advanced materials (metamaterials, nanomaterials, energetic materials, high-performance composites)
Quantum computing, quantum sensing, or quantum communications
Surveillance, monitoring, or tracking technologies
Cybersecurity, encryption, or offensive cyber capabilities
Data or methods that could lower barriers to creating dangerous agents or materials
items.helpText: Select if your research produces information, tools, or methods that could make it easier for someone to cause harm — even if that is not the intent.

items.label: International collaboration with export-controlled regions
items.helpText: Collaboration with partners in countries subject to export controls, sanctions, or enhanced due diligence requirements.

items.label: Work with potential military or intelligence applications
Research outputs that could enable harm if published in detail
items.helpText: Select if publishing your full methods or results could provide a roadmap for harmful applications, even though the research itself is beneficial.
